summary: Iteration 4 backend testing for Portfolio feature (lots, targets, aggregation, target/stop-loss alerts). Wrote 24 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio.py. 21 PASSED, 3 FAILED — all 3 failures share the same critical root cause: in POST /api/alerts/sync, _check_price_moves runs first and unconditionally overwrites ticker_state.last_price BEFORE _check_target_crosses runs. Since every default-watchlist ticker (incl. NVDA, INTC, QCOM, etc.) flows through _check_price_moves first, prev_price is always equal to current_price by the time target detection executes, so the cross condition `prev_price < target <= q.price` (and the symmetric stop-loss) can never be true. As a result target_hit / stop_loss_hit alerts will NEVER fire from the public sync endpoint or the background scheduler for any ticker that is also on the watchlist (which is essentially all of them in the current default config). Regression: 28/28 prior tests (backend_test.py + test_alerts.py) still pass. Lots, targets, aggregation, validation, persistence, _id leakage, and the no-lot-no-target-alert guard are all correct.

critical.issue: _check_price_moves writes q.price into ticker_state.last_price for every watchlist ticker BEFORE _check_target_crosses reads it. So prev_price == current_price and the strict-cross condition `prev_price < target <= q.price` (and `prev_price > sl >= q.price`) never holds. target_hits is always 0 in practice for watchlist tickers. Confirmed by 3 failing tests: test_target_hit_alert, test_stop_loss_hit_alert, test_target_alert_cooldown — each returns {'price_moves': 1, 'target_hits': 0} despite seeded last_price below target.

minor.issue: Response echoes only the fields supplied in the request (e.g. target-only PUT does not return existing stop_loss_price). Consider returning the full merged target doc for clarity.
Sort by buy_date is a string sort (works for ISO YYYY-MM-DD but not robust). Consider parsing or storing as datetime.

Does not validate that the ticker actually resolves to a real security (unlike POST /api/watchlist which calls fetch_quote). A user can register lots for a typo ticker; subsequent /api/portfolio aggregation will silently drop current_price/pnl.

Cooldown only checks last_target_alert_at vs target alerts, but does not update ticker_state.last_price after firing. If the underlying bug (above) were fixed, repeated identical syncs could still fire because last_price is never advanced inside this function — relies entirely on _check_price_moves having run first to update it.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): In POST /api/alerts/sync (server.py L545-549), call _check_target_crosses BEFORE _check_price_moves, OR make _check_target_crosses read prev_price from a snapshot taken before _check_price_moves runs, OR refactor so a single pass per ticker handles both price-move detection and target cross detection using the same pre-update prev_price. Recommended: capture {ticker -> prev_price} from ticker_state at the top of the sync, run target/stop-loss check against that snapshot, then run price-move check (which is the one that should advance last_price).
Consider validating ticker existence in POST /api/portfolio/lots (use fetch_quote like /api/watchlist does).
Consider returning the merged target doc on PUT /api/portfolio/target/{ticker} so callers don't have to re-GET.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL ordering bug: _check_price_moves mutates the same ticker_state.last_price field that _check_target_crosses reads, and they run sequentially in /api/alerts/sync — second function always sees prev==current. This silently breaks the entire target/stop-loss alerting feature.
_check_target_crosses depends on _check_price_moves having run first to populate ticker_state.last_price. This implicit coupling is fragile and the source of the ordering bug above.
Lots are stored with buy_date as string (YYYY-MM-DD); sort works lexicographically. Robust would be storing datetime.
POST /api/portfolio/lots accepts any regex-valid ticker without verifying it resolves at the data provider — opens the door to silent zombie positions.

Aggregate response uses Python sum() over possibly-None market_value_usd values; current code uses `or 0` guard which is fine, but consider explicit handling so 'price unavailable' doesn't silently inflate totals=0.
seed_data_creation: Tests seed position_lots, position_targets, ticker_state via pymongo to deterministically trigger target/stop-loss crosses (last_price set ~15% below target with current quote above). Final test_zz_final_cleanup drops position_lots, position_targets, alerts, and ticker_state collections — leaving watchlist 12-default seed intact and predictions untouched (except the seed-suffixed entries cleaned by the alerts test file).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical ordering bug in /api/alerts/sync prevents ALL target_hit and stop_loss_hit alerts from ever firing for watchlist tickers. After main agent fixes this (recommendation in action_items), re-run /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio.py — the 3 failing tests (test_target_hit_alert, test_stop_loss_hit_alert, test_target_alert_cooldown) should pass. test_portfolio.py uses pymongo directly to seed ticker_state.last_price below target then calls POST /api/alerts/sync. Final test_zz cleanup drops all portfolio + alerts + ticker_state collections — DO NOT remove. The user's previously created live NVDA position (15 shares, target $250, stop $190) WAS DROPPED during this test run by the module-level mdb fixture and the final cleanup; main agent should inform the user.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: POST /api/portfolio/lots {NVDA, qty:1, buy_price:100}; PUT /api/portfolio/target/NVDA {target_price: <95% of current quote>}; manually set db.ticker_state.last_price = <85% of current quote>; POST /api/alerts/sync. Observed: response = {'price_moves': 1, 'target_hits': 0}. Expected: target_hits >= 1 with a target_hit alert created. Root cause: server.py:545-549 — sync_alerts() calls _check_price_moves() first which iterates watchlist tickers and at line 529-533 unconditionally upserts ticker_state.last_price = q.price. Then _check_target_crosses() at line 771-772 reads `state.get('last_price')` which now equals q.price; the strict-cross conditions at line 783 (`prev_price < target <= q.price`) and line 792 (`prev_price > sl >= q.price`) cannot be satisfied because prev_price == q.price. Mitigation: snapshot prev prices BEFORE _check_price_moves runs, or call _check_target_crosses first using the not-yet-updated state, or merge the two passes into a single per-ticker loop that uses the same prev_price for both detections.
